question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a scenario via the `scenarios` map with a `properties` entry setting `maxTransactionFee` to `Long.MAX_VALUE` (9223372036854775807), bypassing the absence of an upper-bound constraint on `maxTransactionFee`, causing the operator account to authorize transactions with astronomically high fee caps that drain operator funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a scenario `properties` map with key `maxTransactionFee` set to `0` as a string, exploiting Jackson's `convertValue` with `ACCEPT_CASE_INSENSITIVE_PROPERTIES=true` to bypass the `@Min(1)` constraint on the supplier, causing transactions to be submitted with a zero fee cap and disrupting network operations?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario `properties` entry with key `MAXTRANSACTIONFEE` (all caps) to exploit `MapperFeature.ACCEPT_CASE_INSENSITIVE_PROPERTIES=true` in `ConfigurableTransactionGenerator`, bypassing any case-sensitive validation and injecting an out-of-design fee value into the `TransactionSupplier`?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `maxTransactionFee` in the `properties` map to a negative long string (e.g., `-1`), exploiting the lack of a lower-bound check beyond `@Min(1)` in the supplier, causing `Hbar.fromTinybars(-1)` to be passed to the SDK and resulting in undefined fee behavior or transaction rejection?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a scenario with `properties` containing `maxTransactionFee=1000000000000` (exceeding the 1_000_000_000 default by 1000x), exploiting the absence of a maximum constraint on `maxTransactionFee` in all `TransactionSupplier` implementations, causing the operator to authorize fee payments far outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user add multiple scenarios to the `scenarios` map each with `maxTransactionFee` set to `Long.MAX_VALUE`, exploiting the lack of aggregate fee cap validation in `validate()`, causing concurrent high-fee transactions across all `clients` (up to the `clients` count) to simultaneously drain operator funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `tps` to a very high value (e.g., 10000.0) in a scenario with `maxTransactionFee` set to the maximum long value, exploiting the absence of a combined fee-rate cap in `PublishProperties`, causing the operator account to be drained at an accelerated rate through high-frequency high-fee transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `clients` to its maximum feasible integer value while also setting `maxTransactionFee` to a large value in each scenario, exploiting the multiplicative effect of concurrent clients on fee spending, causing the operator account to be exhausted faster than the design intends?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `recordPercent=1.0` in a scenario, forcing every transaction to trigger a `TransactionRecordQuery` with a hardcoded `Hbar.from(1, HbarUnit.HBAR)` payment in `TransactionPublisher`, effectively doubling the fee expenditure per transaction outside the `maxTransactionFee` design parameter?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set both `receiptPercent=1.0` and `recordPercent=1.0` in a scenario, causing every transaction to incur both a receipt query and a record query cost, multiplying the effective fee expenditure beyond what the `maxTransactionFee` field alone controls?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `batchDivisor` to its minimum value of `100` while maximizing `tps` and `maxTransactionFee`, exploiting the interaction between batch size and fee rate to submit the maximum number of high-fee transactions per time window?
